---
title: ""
toc: false
lightbox: true
---


:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="49%"}

![](photos/headshot.png)

:::

::: {.column width="5%"}
<!-- Blank -->
:::

::: {.column width="46%"}

<!-- I'm [Ed Rubin](bio.html), an associate professor in the [Department of Economics](https://economics.uoregon.edu) at the [University of Oregon](https://www.uoregon.edu). -->

[Hayatullah Ahmadzai]{style="font-size: 1.8em; color: #34495E; font-weight: 600;"}
<br>
[Agricultural Economist]{style="color: #3d4f63; font-weight: 600;"}
<br>
Durham, NC, USA 

<!--
Academic CV Color Palette (saved options)

#1F4E79  - Academic Navy (US econ standard, safest choice)
#2C3E50  - Slate Blue-Gray (modern, clean, slightly softer navy)
#34495E  - Dark Blue-Gray (minimalist, neutral academic tone)
#3A6EA5  - Steel Blue (slightly more personality, still professional)
#222222  - Near Black (ultra conservative, print-safe)
#4CAF50  - Template Green (original default, tech/web feel)
#163729  - Forest Academic Green (your LaTeX-style deep green)
-->

<!--
My research investigates how climate shocks, agricultural and food policy, and market conditions affect farm and rural household outcomes and resilience in fragile and resource-constrained settings, including developing-country contexts, using applied econometrics, causal inference, and impact evaluation methods to support evidence-based policy and program design.
-->

My research investigates how climate shocks, agricultural and food policy, and market conditions affect farm and rural household outcomes and resilience. Drawing on methods from applied econometrics, causal inference, impact evaluation, and data science, I produce empirical evidence to inform policy, program design, and implementation, with much of my work focusing on fragile and resource-constrained contexts.

### Recent Work

**[Climate Shocks, Household Food Security and Welfare in Afghanistan](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.foodpol.2025.102910)**
<br>Ahmadzai, H., and Morrissey, O. *Food Policy*, 2025. Also presented at [Stanford University, April 2026](https://events.stanford.edu/event/climate-shocks).

**[Crop Diversification and Farm Household Welfare during Conflict: Afghanistan 2011–2017](https://doi.org/10.1080/00220388.2024.2404576)**
<br>Ahmadzai, H., and Morrissey, O. *The Journal of Development Studies*, 2025, 61(3), 381–399.


### Research Interests
Agricultural & Food Policy • Climate Change, Adaptation, & Agricultural Resilience • Agribusiness & Farm Economics • Food Security, Rural Livelihoods & Agrifood Systems • Applied Econometrics & Impact Evaluation


Affiliations: External Research Fellow, CREDIT, University of Nottingham | MESA Global Academy Scholar (2025–2027), Middle East Studies Association

<!--
### about me

 I'm an environmental economist who researches how human behavior (individual and policy) affects social equity and the health of the natural world.

As an empiricist, I tackle these issues with a toolbox that draws from econometrics, statistics, and data science.

Examples from a few recent projects…
<br>↳ [*unequal health impacts from glyphosate exposure*](https://www.pnas.org/doi/10.1073/pnas.2413013121)
<br>↳ [*curiously missing data in air-quality monitors*](https://doi.org/10.1162/rest_a_01477)
<br>↳ [*disparate wildfire smoke avoidance*](Papers/draft-smoke.pdf)
<br>↳ [*a field experiment in customer-based gender bias*](https://www.journals.uchicago.edu/doi/abs/10.1086/733049)
<br>↳ [*pitfalls of machine learning in a 2SLS framework*](https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2505.13422)
<br>↳ [*strategic location of coal-fueled power plants*](Papers/draft-plant-locations.pdf). -->



### Contact

Email: ahmadzaihayat@outlook.com 
<br> 
[Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=iTDaZLcAAAAJ&hl=en) | [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ahmadzaihayat)

<!--
### me

*Passions:* [the](Gallery/20250101-IMG_4248.jpg){.lightbox} [ou](Gallery/20250215-IMG_5072){.lightbox}t[do](Gallery/20250827-IMG_0238.jpg){.lightbox}o[rs](Gallery/20240629-IMG_8522_jpg.jpg){.lightbox}, [bikes](Gallery/20241221-IMG_3497_jpg.jpg){.lightbox}, [good](Gallery/20241227-IMG_4034_jpg.jpg){.lightbox} [food](Gallery/20251102-IMG_0950.jpg){.lightbox}/[coffee](Gallery/20250731-IMG_9226.jpg){.lightbox}/[drink](Gallery/20250307-IMG_5581.jpg){.lightbox}[s](Gallery/20250328-IMG_6463.jpg){.lightbox}, [road](Gallery/20250628-IMG_7806.jpg){.lightbox} [trips](Gallery/20250628-IMG_7797.jpg){.lightbox} [beauty](Gallery/20250629-IMG_7900.jpg){.lightbox}/[photography](photos.html), data, and (most of all) [loving](Gallery/20240806-IMG_0409_jpg.jpg){.lightbox} [people](Gallery/20250726-IMG_9131.jpg){.lightbox}.

### more

A few other professional items… I
<br>↳ *started [TWEEDS](https://tweeds.io) with [friends](https://tweeds.io/about/)*,
<br>↳ *help run [OSWEET](https://edrub.in/osweet.html)*,
<br>↳ *co-wrote a textbook,*<br>[↳]{style="color: white;"} *[Data Science and Public Policy](https://link.springer.com/book/10.1007/978-3-030-71352-2)*.

Please look around or [contact me](contact.html). -->



:::

::::
